feature: AIOS Developer Experience — Phase 3 (ADR gap closure)
description: Close remaining gaps against ADR-2604131500: hex new command, decision defaults+reasoning, configurable deadlines, trust floor enforcement, hex override/pause commands.

phases.name: Decision quality — defaults, reasoning, configurable deadlines
phases.description: Make decisions self-documenting with defaults, reasoning, and configurable auto-resolution deadlines.

tasks.name: Add default_action and reasoning fields to decision surfacing
tasks.description: When the workplan executor surfaces a decision (P3.2 trust check in workplan_executor.rs), include default_action (what hex will do if no response), reasoning (why hex chose this default — reference trust level, architectural rules, or taste preferences), and deadline_at (when auto-resolution fires). Store these in the inbox notification payload as structured JSON. Update the briefing renderer to display them: 'Default: approve in 2h. Reason: trust level is suggest for adapters/secondary.'

tasks.name: Configurable decision deadline
tasks.description: Read decision deadline from: (1) HEX_DECISION_DEADLINE_SECS env var, (2) .hex/project.json → decision.deadline_secs, (3) default 7200 (2h per ADR). Update cleanup.rs decision auto-resolution to use this instead of hardcoded 4h. Also expose as hex trust config --deadline <seconds>.

phases.name: Trust floor enforcement
phases.description: Destructive operations never auto-elevate past act.

tasks.name: Enforce trust ceiling for destructive scopes
tasks.description: In hex-nexus/src/routes/trust.rs set_trust(), when the scope is 'deployment' or 'dependencies', reject elevation to 'silent'. Return 400 with message: 'Trust floor: destructive scopes (deployment, dependencies) cannot be elevated to silent. Maximum: act.' Also prevent self-elevation in the decay reducer — only humans can elevate, never the system.

phases.name: hex new — structured project intake
phases.description: Interactive command for onboarding a new project into hex.

tasks.name: Add hex new command
tasks.description: Add NewAction to hex-cli with subcommand 'hex new <path>'. Implementation: (1) validate path exists or create it, (2) run hex init in that directory (reuse existing init logic), (3) register project via POST /api/projects/register, (4) seed trust at suggest level (auto via P4.2), (5) prompt for initial taste preferences interactively or accept --taste-from <project> to copy from existing, (6) print summary: 'Project <name> registered. Trust: all suggest. Run hex brief to check status.' Non-interactive mode with --name, --description flags for CI/scripted use.

phases.name: hex override + hex pause — Layer 4 emergency controls
phases.description: Direct intervention commands for the 1% emergency case.

tasks.name: Add hex pause command
tasks.description: Add PauseAction to hex-cli: 'hex pause [project]' calls POST /api/workplan/pause for the active workplan execution. If no active execution, prints 'Nothing running.' Prints confirmation: 'Paused: <feature>. Resume with hex resume.' Also add 'hex resume [project]' that calls POST /api/workplan/resume.

tasks.name: Add hex override command
tasks.description: Add OverrideAction to hex-cli: 'hex override <project> <instruction>' sends a priority-2 notification to all agents working on that project via POST /api/inbox/notify with kind='override'. The workplan executor checks for override notifications at each phase boundary (already has steering check — extend it). On override: pause current work, execute the override instruction, then resume. Example: 'hex override hex-intf "Revert the last commit on adapters/secondary and re-run tests"'.

phases.name: MCP tools + ADR compliance verification
phases.description: Wire remaining MCP tools and verify full ADR compliance.

tasks.name: Wire MCP tools for new, pause, override
tasks.description: Add hex_new, hex_pause, hex_resume, hex_override MCP tools to mcp.rs. Update all 3 JSON definition files.

tasks.name: ADR compliance smoke test
tasks.description: Verify all 7 ADR sections are covered: (1) hex brief shows narrative, (2) hex decide resolves with auto-expiry, (3) hex steer classifies directives, (4) hex trust show/elevate/reduce/pin/history works, (5) hex taste list/set/forget/pin + prompt injection, (6) hex new creates project with trust seeding, (7) hex pause/resume/override work. Print pass/fail per section.
